questions.q: What occasion did the great tournament at Hastinapura celebrate?
options: The completion of the princes' weapons training
questions.explanation: The tournament was the graduation exhibition staged to display the Kuru princes' mastery of arms at the end of their training.

questions.q: Who arranged and presided over the exhibition of the princes' skills?
questions.explanation: Drona, the princes' weapons master, organized the exhibition to show the court what his pupils had learned.

questions.q: Which unknown warrior strode into the arena and stunned the crowd?
questions.explanation: Karna, then a stranger to the court, entered unannounced in his golden armor and earrings and electrified the arena.

questions.q: Whose feats did the stranger repeat one by one before the crowd?
questions.explanation: Karna matched every feat Arjuna had just performed, proving himself the equal of the day's champion.

questions.q: What did the stranger demand after matching the day's champion?
questions.explanation: Karna challenged Arjuna to a duel then and there, setting up the rivalry that would run through the entire epic.

questions.q: Who crowned the stranger a king on the spot in the arena?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana, seeing both a rival to Arjuna and a friend worth winning, crowned Karna king of Anga in a single theatrical gesture.

questions.q: Over which kingdom was Karna crowned at the tournament?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana bestowed the eastern kingdom of Anga on Karna, which gave Karna the royal rank needed to face a prince.

questions.q: Why was Karna mocked in the arena?
options: He was thought a charioteer's son
He refused to name his teacher
questions.explanation: Karna was believed to be the son of the charioteer Adhiratha, a birth the princes held unworthy of a duel with royalty.

questions.q: What brought the confrontation between Karna and Arjuna to an end that day?
questions.explanation: Sunset closed the tournament before the duel could be fought, leaving the challenge between Karna and Arjuna forever unfinished.

questions.q: What lasting prize did Duryodhana win by his gift at the tournament?
questions.explanation: By crowning Karna at his moment of humiliation, Duryodhana bought a loyalty Karna would honor until his death.

questions.q: Who demanded that the unknown challenger declare his lineage before dueling?
questions.explanation: Kripa invoked the rule that princes duel only their equals and asked Karna to name his father, mother, and royal line.

questions.q: Which Pandava taunted Karna that he deserved a whip rather than a duel?
questions.explanation: Bhima jeered that as a charioteer's son Karna should take up the whip, an insult Karna never forgot.

questions.q: Who was sent to separate Bhima and Duryodhana when their mace bout turned dangerous?
questions.explanation: When the mace duel between Bhima and Duryodhana grew truly hostile, Drona sent his son Ashwatthama to part them.

questions.q: Who recognized the stranger by his divine armor and earrings and fainted in the stands?
questions.explanation: Kunti knew the golden armor and earrings of the son she had set adrift, and the sight of him facing Arjuna made her swoon.

questions.q: Who attended to Kunti when she collapsed during the tournament?
questions.explanation: Vidura had Kunti revived with sandal-water and comforted her, though her secret remained unspoken.

questions.q: Whose arrival in the arena exposed Karna's humble upbringing?
questions.explanation: The old charioteer Adhiratha tottered into the arena, and Karna's greeting revealed him before the whole assembly as a suta's son.

questions.q: What did Karna do the moment Adhiratha entered the arena?
options: He bowed to him as his father
He turned his face away in shame
He denied knowing the old man
He led him quietly out of sight
questions.explanation: His head still wet from the coronation, Karna saluted Adhiratha as his father, an act of love that handed his mockers their weapon.

questions.q: How did Duryodhana answer the mockery of Karna's birth?
options: By offering to adopt Karna as a brother
By declaring that the origins of heroes and rivers are unknown
By producing proof of Karna's royal blood
By challenging Bhima to fight him instead
questions.explanation: Duryodhana declared that the sources of rivers and the lineage of heroes are alike hidden, and that valor is its own credential.

questions.q: When Karna asked what he could give in return for the crown of Anga, what did Duryodhana request?
options: The divine armor on Karna's body
questions.explanation: Duryodhana asked only for Karna's friendship, and Karna granted it for life.

questions.q: Who described the day's dramatic events to the blind king Dhritarashtra?
questions.explanation: At the tournament it was Vidura who told Dhritarashtra what the roaring crowd was watching, including Karna's entrance.

questions.q: Under whose royal sanction did Duryodhana bestow a kingdom in the arena?
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra approved his son's gift on the spot, lending the coronation of Karna full royal authority.

questions.q: Where was the tournament of the Kuru princes staged?
options: A great arena built outside Hastinapura
The riverside ghats of the Ganga
questions.explanation: A vast arena with galleries for the royal household and the public was specially built on level ground at Hastinapura.

questions.q: Which feat did Arjuna perform with five arrows during his display?
options: Pinning five birds with one draw
Shooting them into the mouth of a moving iron boar
Cutting five banners from distant poles
questions.explanation: Among his exhibition feats, Arjuna sank five arrows into the jaws of a moving iron boar.

questions.q: Into what target did Arjuna sink twenty-one arrows during the exhibition?
options: The eye of a revolving fish
A mustard seed on a post
The gap of a swinging ring
The hollow of a cow's horn swinging on a rope
questions.explanation: Arjuna shot twenty-one arrows into the hollow of a cow's horn that hung swinging from a rope.

questions.q: What omen split the sky as Karna and Arjuna faced each other in the arena?
options: Indra's clouds shaded Arjuna while the sun blazed on Karna
An eclipse darkened the whole arena
A rain of flowers fell between them
questions.explanation: The rival fathers showed themselves: Indra's clouds gathered over Arjuna while Surya's light bathed Karna, dividing the heavens like the crowd below.

questions.q: How was Karna consecrated king in the arena?
options: Bhishma placed his own circlet on Karna's head
Brahmins anointed him with holy water on a golden seat
Duryodhana simply proclaimed it without rites
Dhritarashtra sent the crown of Anga from the palace
questions.explanation: Duryodhana had brahmins perform a full consecration on the spot, seating Karna on gold and anointing him with sacred water, parched rice, and flowers.

questions.q: What exactly did Bhima say a charioteer's son deserved instead of a duel?
questions.explanation: Bhima sneered that the whip suited Karna's hands better than a warrior's challenge, adding that he no more deserved Anga than a dog deserves the sacrificial offering.

questions.q: How did Duryodhana counter Kripa's objection that princes duel only their equals?
options: He claimed Karna was secretly of royal blood
He offered to duel Arjuna in Karna's place
He cited that heroes and leaders of armies may claim kingship, then crowned Karna
He demanded the rule be struck from the warrior code
questions.explanation: Duryodhana argued that kingship belongs to the well-born, the hero, and the leader of armies, then solved the objection by making Karna a king himself.

questions.q: How did the watching crowd react to the standoff between Karna and Arjuna?
options: It split into rival factions cheering each champion
It pelted Karna with flowers alone
questions.explanation: The assembly divided in tumult, some shouting for Karna and some for Arjuna, mirroring the war to come.

questions.q: How did Arjuna verbally answer Karna's challenge in the arena?
options: He welcomed a worthy rival at last
He vowed to finish the duel at Kurukshetra
He asked Karna to join the Pandavas
He scorned him as an uninvited intruder
questions.explanation: Arjuna retorted that Karna had entered uninvited and would meet the fate reserved for unbidden meddlers.
